meta_title: For Founders: Git with AI to Solve no portfolio (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome no portfolio. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Git with AI to Solve no portfolio (Advanced Checklist)
content.seo_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome no portfolio. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code to build SaaS assets that convert.
content.answer_block: Stop waiting for clients. Use Git-driven AI workflows to build high-value, deployable SaaS micro-products in hours, not weeks. By leveraging Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative refinement, you transform your repository into a living portfolio of production-grade code that proves your technical authority to potential investors and customers.
content.intro:
The cold reality of the SaaS market is that nobody cares about your ideas; they care about your execution. If you lack a portfolio, you are invisible. You aren't just missing projects; you are missing the trust required to close high-ticket deals. The gap between 'no portfolio' and 'proven founder' is not a four-year degree—it is the speed at which you ship working, revenue-generating software.

We are shifting the paradigm by treating Git as an AI-augmented engine. By integrating Antigravity’s structural precision with the reasoning depth of Claude Code, you can architect, build, and document professional-grade micro-SaaS products in a single weekend. This isn't just coding; it's building a digital legacy that functions as a 24/7 sales funnel for your technical capability.

steps.title: Define the High-Value MVP
steps.description: Identify a specific, painful problem in a niche market. Use AI to scope a micro-SaaS that solves one problem perfectly, ensuring the scope is small enough to finish in 48 hours but complex enough to showcase architecture.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a CTO. Define a micro-SaaS architecture for [Problem]. Focus on a lean stack: Next.js, Supabase, and Stripe. Detail the schema, core API routes, and critical business logic required for a functional MVP.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a project roadmap with a file structure for a Next.js MVP that solves [Problem]'

steps.title: Scaffold the Repository
steps.description: Initialize your project with a production-ready folder structure. Speed is the priority, but maintainability is the long-term value.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate the boilerplate code for a production-ready Next.js app. Include authentication hooks, database client setup, and a clean folder structure for scalability.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize the repo with the proposed structure and install essential dependencies'

steps.title: Implement Core Logic via AI
steps.description: Build the primary value driver. Use Claude Code to write the backend services that handle the user's core task, ensuring type safety and error handling.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the server-side logic for [Core Feature]. Ensure it includes input validation, database transaction handling, and robust error logging.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the backend API route for [Core Feature] including type-safe database calls'

steps.title: Optimize for Performance & SEO
steps.description: A portfolio project is useless if it's slow. Use AI to audit your code for performance bottlenecks and implement SEO best practices.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the codebase for performance bottlenecks. Suggest improvements for rendering speed, database query optimization, and SEO metadata implementation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Audit the codebase for performance and implement SEO best practices for the landing page'

steps.title: Document and Deploy
steps.description: Document your code as if you were handing it off to a team of engineers. This documentation is your strongest proof of professional competence.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a comprehensive README.md that explains the technical architecture, the problem solved, and how to scale this project.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a professional README.md and write a deployment script for Vercel'

pro_tips: Always commit your AI-generated code with descriptive, conventional commits to show a professional development history.
Include a 'Business Logic' folder in your repo; it proves you think about revenue, not just syntax.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your critical path; nothing screams 'Senior Engineer' like 80% test coverage.
Add a 'Decision Log' markdown file to explain why you chose specific libraries; it shows architectural maturity.
Deploy every single iteration to a live URL; a working link is worth 10,000 lines of GitHub code.
content.next_steps: Once your first project is deployed, repeat this process in a different niche. Build three distinct micro-SaaS products to create a diverse 'Technical Portfolio' that you can present to future clients or employers.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark and start building for the market. Leverage the tools that elite founders use to scale their output and prove their worth. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
